title: Fix Regular Text Rendering on Help Documentation Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the help documentation page to render regular body text correctly, so that I can read the documentation without missing content.
description:
Regular body text on the documentation page at `/documentation` was not rendering. The page appeared to skip or hide standard paragraph text, making the help content unreadable.

## Fix
- Corrected the CSS/rendering issue causing body text to be invisible
- All documentation links now open in a new tab for better navigation
acceptance_criteria: Regular body text renders correctly on the documentation page
All documentation links open in a new browser tab
Page layout and formatting are consistent across documentation sections
ux_changes: Fixed CSS rendering issue that prevented regular body text from displaying on the help documentation page. Documentation links updated to open in new tabs.
implementation_notes: Root cause was a CSS issue affecting paragraph text rendering on the documentation page. Fix involved correcting the CSS rules. As part of the fix, all outbound links were updated to use target="_blank" to open in new tabs.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the rendering issue and pushed to staging. Links now open in new tabs. Product owner confirmed the fix looked correct.
